## [00:00 - 03:02]

Operation Quick Start, this is the mental game part two, which is all about how I keep my mental game intact. So I talked about mental game or your inner game and how that's the absolute most important thing for your success as an entrepreneur. It's not what's happening outside your body, really, outside of your mind. It's not the external factors. It's not what your competitors are doing, what the market is doing, what the economy is doing. It's what's happening inside your head and staying at a high mental state so that you can continue to create, that you can continue to overcome, that you can keep moving forward, that you can keep your momentum, that you can regain your momentum if you lose it, that you can deal with setbacks. That's what mental game is and it is the most important thing, the most important factor in your success. In your getting started and in your maintaining, getting momentum, maintaining momentum and then just having the stamina to keep your business going. It's been over 20 years for me and the one thing I can, I feel good about telling you is that I've got really good stamina and I can keep on grinding away and the reason I keep grinding away, I can keep on grinding away, is because I work really hard on my mental game. I work really hard on my inner game and I've built in a lot of things within my life to keep my mental game, if not at a peak, at least at a very high level and that's what this section is about. So one of the most important things and I'm coming up on 10 years where I've really focused on this is my morning ritual. So every morning when I wake up, I have this a ritual, I have these habits that I go through that set me up for a really strong powerful day. So the first thing I do or what really the first thing I do is is oral hygiene, brushing my teeth and flossing my teeth and hydrating, drinking 16 ounces of water. Then, so I mean it's simple but you know right now there's so much research coming out about the importance of oral hygiene and for your entire body and part of your mental game is staying healthy. If you're not healthy then if you're getting colds, if you're getting sick, if you're getting a flu, it's you just can't create, you can't stay on top of your game if you're getting sick. So I'm a big fan of oral hygiene, staying hydrated and then right after that I go right into yoga. I do about 15 to 20 minutes very light yoga,

write down what's happening in my day maybe that's something I reflect back on but in the morning it's just sort about clearing your mind. Another habit that I have is what I call the three wins and I learned this from my coach Dan Sullivan and and I do this a lot of times I'll do it when I go to bed most the time when I go to bed sometimes when I wake up and what I do is I write down three wins I had that day and then three wins I'm going to have the next day or if I'm doing in the morning I it's three wins I had yesterday and
